title: McMillan Township, Luce County, Michigan

infobox.value: is a [civil township](/wiki/Civil_township "Civil township") of [Luce County](/wiki/Luce_County,_Michigan "Luce County, Michigan") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). The population was 2,471 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[2]](#cite_note-C2020-2) At 588.78 square miles (1,524.9 km2) of total land area, McMillan Township is the [largest municipality by area](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_in_Michigan "List of municipalities in Michigan") in the state of Michigan.

is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area "Unincorporated area") located along the [Tahquamenon River](/wiki/Tahquamenon_River "Tahquamenon River") at [46°24′02″N 85°16′40″W﻿ / ﻿46.40056°N 85.27778°W﻿ / 46.40056; -85.27778](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_24_02_N_85_16_40_W_).[[6]](#cite_note-6) The landing is the northern terminus of a private railroad spur from Soo Junction, which now operates as the Toonerville Trolley Riverboat Tour offering tours on the 5.5 miles (8.9 km) 24-inch [gauge](/wiki/Rail_gauge "Rail gauge") railroad. The branch was built by the [Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railroad](/wiki/Duluth,_South_Shore_and_Atlantic_Railroad "Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railroad") from Soo Junction to the Hunter & Love Lumber Company mill on the Tahquamenon River in 1911. Built in [standard gauge](/wiki/Standard_gauge "Standard gauge"), the line was converted to [narrow gauge](/wiki/Narrow_gauge "Narrow gauge") to accommodate the mining engines used to pull the trolley tour's cars by Joseph Beech, Sr., founder of the riverboat tours.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

is an unincorporated community located at [46°40′28″N 85°36′59″W﻿ / ﻿46.67444°N 85.61639°W﻿ / 46.67444; -85.61639](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_40_28_N_85_36_59_W_).[[7]](#cite_note-7)

is an unincorporated community just west of Newberry at [46°21′05″N 85°32′31″W﻿ / ﻿46.35139°N 85.54194°W﻿ / 46.35139; -85.54194](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_21_05_N_85_32_31_W_).[[8]](#cite_note-8)

is an unincorporated community located along [H-37](/wiki/H-37_(Michigan_county_highway) "H-37 (Michigan county highway)") in the western portion of the township at [46°25′08″N 85°35′38″W﻿ / ﻿46.41889°N 85.59389°W﻿ / 46.41889; -85.59389](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_25_08_N_85_35_38_W_).[[9]](#cite_note-9)

is an unincorporated community located at the intersection of H-37 and M-123 at [46°25′09″N 85°30′38″W﻿ / ﻿46.41917°N 85.51056°W﻿ / 46.41917; -85.51056](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_25_09_N_85_30_38_W_).[[10]](#cite_note-10) It located about 4.0 miles (6.4 km) north of Newberry.[[11]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERomig1986207-11)
is a former lumbering settlement located between Newberry and Soo Junction.[[12]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERomig1986279-12)

is a village centered along [M-123](/wiki/M-123_(Michigan_highway) "M-123 (Michigan highway)") at [46°21′08″N 85°30′38″W﻿ / ﻿46.35222°N 85.51056°W﻿ / 46.35222; -85.51056](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_21_08_N_85_30_38_W_).[[13]](#cite_note-13)

is an unincorporated community located along the railway line just east of Newberry at [46°21′01″N 85°25′46″W﻿ / ﻿46.35028°N 85.42944°W﻿ / 46.35028; -85.42944](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_21_01_N_85_25_46_W_).[[14]](#cite_note-14)

is an unincorporated community located along the Tahquamenon River at [46°22′20″N 85°25′12″W﻿ / ﻿46.37222°N 85.42000°W﻿ / 46.37222; -85.42000](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_22_20_N_85_25_12_W_).[[15]](#cite_note-15)

is an unincorporated community located along the railway line just west of Dollarville at [46°20′49″N 85°34′39″W﻿ / ﻿46.34694°N 85.57750°W﻿ / 46.34694; -85.57750](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_20_49_N_85_34_39_W_).[[16]](#cite_note-16)

is an unincorporated community located along Deer Park Road between Deer Park and Newberry at [46°34′09″N 85°35′29″W﻿ / ﻿46.56917°N 85.59139°W﻿ / 46.56917; -85.59139](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_34_09_N_85_35_29_W_).[[17]](#cite_note-17)

is an unincorporated community located east of Newberry at [46°20′08″N 85°15′34″W﻿ / ﻿46.33556°N 85.25944°W﻿ / 46.33556; -85.25944](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_20_08_N_85_15_34_W_).[[18]](#cite_note-18) The community was named for its location along a railway junction of the South Shore and Atlantic Railroad and had its own post office from January 19, 1891 until May 30, 1941.[[19]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERomig1986523-19)

**McMillan Township** is a [civil township](/wiki/Civil_township "Civil township") of [Luce County](/wiki/Luce_County,_Michigan "Luce County, Michigan") in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [Michigan](/wiki/Michigan "Michigan"). The population was 2,471 at the [2020 census](/wiki/2020_United_States_census "2020 United States census").[[2]](#cite_note-C2020-2) At 588.78 square miles (1,524.9 km2) of total land area, McMillan Township is the [largest municipality by area](/wiki/List_of_municipalities_in_Michigan "List of municipalities in Michigan") in the state of Michigan.

McMillan Township occupies 65.5% of Luce County's land area and also contains the village of [Newberry](/wiki/Newberry,_Michigan "Newberry, Michigan"), which is the county seat and only incorporated municipality in the county. The township contains two state parks: [Muskallonge Lake State Park](/wiki/Muskallonge_Lake_State_Park "Muskallonge Lake State Park") and the western portion of [Tahquamenon Falls State Park](/wiki/Tahquamenon_Falls_State_Park "Tahquamenon Falls State Park"), which also includes the Upper [Tahquamenon Falls](/wiki/Tahquamenon_Falls "Tahquamenon Falls"). [Crisp Point Light](/wiki/Crisp_Point_Light "Crisp Point Light") is also located in the northeast corner of the township.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

According to the [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/United_States_Census_Bureau "United States Census Bureau"), the township has a total area of 604.35 square miles (1,565.26 km2), of which 588.78 square miles (1,524.93 km2) is land and 15.57 square miles (40.33 km2) (2.58%) is water.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The township has a long shoreline along [Lake Superior](/wiki/Lake_Superior "Lake Superior") and also includes numerous other lakes and rivers, such as [Blind Sucker River](/wiki/Blind_Sucker_River "Blind Sucker River"), [Carp River](/wiki/Carp_River_(Luce_County,_Michigan) "Carp River (Luce County, Michigan)"), [Little Two Hearted River](/wiki/Little_Two_Hearted_River "Little Two Hearted River"), [Tahquamenon River](/wiki/Tahquamenon_River "Tahquamenon River"), and [Two Hearted River](/wiki/Two_Hearted_River "Two Hearted River"), as well as [Muskallonge Lake](/wiki/Muskallonge_Lake "Muskallonge Lake").

The [North Country Trail](/wiki/North_Country_Trail "North Country Trail") also passes through the township.

### Major highways

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Major highways")]

* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/96/M-123_rectangle.svg/40px-M-123_rectangle.svg.png) [M-123](/wiki/M-123_(Michigan_highway) "M-123 (Michigan highway)") runs north–south through the south-central portion of the township.
* ![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/ba/County_H-58.svg/40px-County_H-58.svg.png) [H-58](/wiki/H-58_(Michigan_county_highway) "H-58 (Michigan county highway)"), known locally as Deer Park Road, runs along the shores of Lake Superior and connects to [Pictured Rocks](/wiki/Pictured_Rocks_National_Lakeshore "Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore").

### Communities

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Communities")]

* **Betty B Landing** is an [unincorporated community](/wiki/Unincorporated_area "Unincorporated area") located along the [Tahquamenon River](/wiki/Tahquamenon_River "Tahquamenon River") at [46°24′02″N 85°16′40″W﻿ / ﻿46.40056°N 85.27778°W﻿ / 46.40056; -85.27778](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_24_02_N_85_16_40_W_).[[6]](#cite_note-6) The landing is the northern terminus of a private railroad spur from Soo Junction, which now operates as the Toonerville Trolley Riverboat Tour offering tours on the 5.5 miles (8.9 km) 24-inch [gauge](/wiki/Rail_gauge "Rail gauge") railroad. The branch was built by the [Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railroad](/wiki/Duluth,_South_Shore_and_Atlantic_Railroad "Duluth, South Shore and Atlantic Railroad") from Soo Junction to the Hunter & Love Lumber Company mill on the Tahquamenon River in 1911. Built in [standard gauge](/wiki/Standard_gauge "Standard gauge"), the line was converted to [narrow gauge](/wiki/Narrow_gauge "Narrow gauge") to accommodate the mining engines used to pull the trolley tour's cars by Joseph Beech, Sr., founder of the riverboat tours.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]
* **[Deer Park](/wiki/Deer_Park,_Michigan "Deer Park, Michigan")** is an unincorporated community located at [46°40′28″N 85°36′59″W﻿ / ﻿46.67444°N 85.61639°W﻿ / 46.67444; -85.61639](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_40_28_N_85_36_59_W_).[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* **[Dollarville](/wiki/Dollarville,_Michigan "Dollarville, Michigan")** is an unincorporated community just west of Newberry at [46°21′05″N 85°32′31″W﻿ / ﻿46.35139°N 85.54194°W﻿ / 46.35139; -85.54194](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_21_05_N_85_32_31_W_).[[8]](#cite_note-8)
* **Eightmile Corner** is an unincorporated community located along [H-37](/wiki/H-37_(Michigan_county_highway) "H-37 (Michigan county highway)") in the western portion of the township at [46°25′08″N 85°35′38″W﻿ / ﻿46.41889°N 85.59389°W﻿ / 46.41889; -85.59389](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_25_08_N_85_35_38_W_).[[9]](#cite_note-9)
* **Fourmile Corner** is an unincorporated community located at the intersection of H-37 and M-123 at [46°25′09″N 85°30′38″W﻿ / ﻿46.41917°N 85.51056°W﻿ / 46.41917; -85.51056](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_25_09_N_85_30_38_W_).[[10]](#cite_note-10) It located about 4.0 miles (6.4 km) north of Newberry.[[11]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERomig1986207-11)
* **Hunter's Mill** is a former lumbering settlement located between Newberry and Soo Junction.[[12]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERomig1986279-12)
* **[Newberry](/wiki/Newberry,_Michigan "Newberry, Michigan")** is a village centered along [M-123](/wiki/M-123_(Michigan_highway) "M-123 (Michigan highway)") at [46°21′08″N 85°30′38″W﻿ / ﻿46.35222°N 85.51056°W﻿ / 46.35222; -85.51056](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_21_08_N_85_30_38_W_).[[13]](#cite_note-13)
* **Lencel** is an unincorporated community located along the railway line just east of Newberry at [46°21′01″N 85°25′46″W﻿ / ﻿46.35028°N 85.42944°W﻿ / 46.35028; -85.42944](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_21_01_N_85_25_46_W_).[[14]](#cite_note-14)
* **McPhees Landing** is an unincorporated community located along the Tahquamenon River at [46°22′20″N 85°25′12″W﻿ / ﻿46.37222°N 85.42000°W﻿ / 46.37222; -85.42000](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_22_20_N_85_25_12_W_).[[15]](#cite_note-15)
* **Natalie** is an unincorporated community located along the railway line just west of Dollarville at [46°20′49″N 85°34′39″W﻿ / ﻿46.34694°N 85.57750°W﻿ / 46.34694; -85.57750](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_20_49_N_85_34_39_W_).[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* **[Pine Stump Junction](/wiki/Pine_Stump_Junction,_Michigan "Pine Stump Junction, Michigan")** is an unincorporated community located along Deer Park Road between Deer Park and Newberry at [46°34′09″N 85°35′29″W﻿ / ﻿46.56917°N 85.59139°W﻿ / 46.56917; -85.59139](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_34_09_N_85_35_29_W_).[[17]](#cite_note-17)
* **Soo Junction** is an unincorporated community located east of Newberry at [46°20′08″N 85°15′34″W﻿ / ﻿46.33556°N 85.25944°W﻿ / 46.33556; -85.25944](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&params=46_20_08_N_85_15_34_W_).[[18]](#cite_note-18) The community was named for its location along a railway junction of the South Shore and Atlantic Railroad and had its own post office from January 19, 1891 until May 30, 1941.[[19]](#cite_note-FOOTNOTERomig1986523-19)

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=McMillan_Township,_Luce_County,_Michigan&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Demographics")]

At the [census](/wiki/Census "Census") of 2010, there were 2,692 people, 1,193 households, and 701 families residing in the township.[[3]](#cite_note-GR2-3) The racial makeup of the township was 82.2% [White](/wiki/White_(U.S._Census) "White (U.S. Census)"), 0.3% [African American](/wiki/African_American_(U.S._Census) "African American (U.S. Census)"), 6.2% [Native American](/wiki/Native_American_(U.S._Census) "Native American (U.S. Census)"), 0.2% [Asian](/wiki/Asian_(U.S._Census) "Asian (U.S. Census)"), 0.03% [Pacific Islander](/wiki/Pacific_Islander_(U.S._Census) "Pacific Islander (U.S. Census)"), 0.79% from [other races](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)"), and 4.1% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 1.6% of the population. In 2020, its population was 2,471.[[2]](#cite_note-C2020-2)

In 2010, there were 2,692 households, out of which 24.1% had children under the age of 18 living with them, 42.7% were [married couples](/wiki/Marriage "Marriage") living together, 11.5% had a female householder with no husband present, and 41.2% were non-families. 55.1% of all households were made up of individuals, and 30.8% had someone living alone who was 65 years of age or older. The average household size was 2.20 and the average family size was 2.84.
